Class PersistentEntityJacksonModule.UriStringDeserializer

java.lang.Object
tools.jackson.databind.ValueDeserializer<Object>
tools.jackson.databind.deser.std.StdDeserializer<Object>
org.springframework.data.rest.webmvc.json.PersistentEntityJacksonModule.UriStringDeserializer
All Implemented Interfaces:
tools.jackson.databind.deser.NullValueProvider, tools.jackson.databind.deser.ValueInstantiator.Gettable
Enclosing class:
PersistentEntityJacksonModule
public static class PersistentEntityJacksonModule.UriStringDeserializer extends tools.jackson.databind.deser.std.StdDeserializer<Object>
Custom StdDeserializer to interpret String values as URIs and resolve them using a UriToEntityConverter.
Since:
5.0
Author:
Oliver Gierke, Valentin Rentschler

    UriStringDeserializer(Class<?> type, UriToEntityConverter converter)

    Modifier and Type
    Method
    Description
    @Nullable Object
    deserialize(tools.jackson.core.JsonParser jp, tools.jackson.databind.DeserializationContext ctxt)
     
    @Nullable Object
    deserializeWithType(tools.jackson.core.JsonParser jp, tools.jackson.databind.DeserializationContext ctxt, tools.jackson.databind.jsontype.TypeDeserializer typeDeserializer)
    Deserialize by ignoring the TypeDeserializer, as URIs will either resolve to null or a concrete instance anyway.

  • Constructor Details

    • UriStringDeserializer

      public UriStringDeserializer(Class<?> type, UriToEntityConverter converter)
      Creates a new PersistentEntityJacksonModule.UriStringDeserializer for the given PersistentProperty using the given UriToEntityConverter.
      Parameters:
      type - must not be null.
      converter - must not be null.

    • deserializeWithType

      public @Nullable Object deserializeWithType(tools.jackson.core.JsonParser jp, tools.jackson.databind.DeserializationContext ctxt, tools.jackson.databind.jsontype.TypeDeserializer typeDeserializer)
      Deserialize by ignoring the TypeDeserializer, as URIs will either resolve to null or a concrete instance anyway.
      Overrides:
      deserializeWithType in class tools.jackson.databind.deser.std.StdDeserializer<Object>
